title: 帷幕
summary:
Blackstone and Boyd Carrington discuss the nature of crime and impulse before touring Nayton Manor, a Tudor-period estate. Boyd explains that his uncle, Sir Evelyn, was a recluse who left Boyd a large inheritance. Boyd shares his personal grief regarding his late wife, who died of alcoholism, and a missed romantic opportunity with a much younger woman. As they walk through the manor and its gardens, Boyd discusses local residents, including Colonel Lethbridge and his business-minded wife, and mentions Norton, a man who prefers birdwatching to hunting.

As days pass at Styles, an atmosphere of unease persists among the guests. Blackstone expresses frustration to Hercule Poirot about being kept in the dark regarding the identity of "X." Poirot reprimands Blackstone, explaining that the investigation is not a game of guessing identities, but a mission to save lives. Poirot clarifies that the primary objective is to determine who the next victim will be. When Blackstone suggests there must be a connection between the victim and X, Poirot explains that the genius of X lies in the fact that the connection is so subtle that it remains undiscovered.
